title: Bekith Mortar and Pestle Sets 18/8 Brushed Stainless Steel Spice Grinder Pill Crusher Molcajete Herb Bowl
description:
About this item
Durable and Stable: Food grade 18/8 stainless steel construction, no mercury, no residual taste, durable and rustproof. Padding on the bottom makes it stay put even more.
Comfortable Grip: Functional design with non-skid base, the pestle feels substantial in your hand.
No Messy: Translucent lid allows you to gauge your strikes while keeping flying debris from making a mess.
Easy to Clean: Just rinse thoroughly under the tap to prepare it for the next use.
Versatile Application: From medical pills, to herbs and spices; from the pharmacy to your kitchen.
reviews_5s:
This is my 2nd purchase. One I bought in 2018 still works great and looks new after these many years. It has great durability and value for money. Easy to clean and maintain.20220301
I bought this more because I've always wanted a mortar and pestle. No real need, but this tool goes back to the stone age. Who wouldn't want one?

This is no stone-age tool mind you. It is a hefty chunk of alloy, and now that I have it, I'm finding all sorts of things I "need" to do with it!

Need to crush some pills to give the dogs their medicine? no problem. Works for kids too. Mix the powdered pills in with whatever will convince the patient to eat (hopefully not the same for dogs as for the kids) :-)

Want to grind down some salt or sugar a little finer than usual? easy! Yes, I could do it in the VitaMix, but if all I need is a small amount, the mortar is great.

Want something that looks interesting sitting on the counter? This thing looks great. It is solid aluminum, a good hefty chunk of it machined out. Probably a lot of waste in the manufacturing process -- if that bothers anyone .

Using it: The bowl of the mortar is big enough for most anything crush-worthy, and the pestle has a nice feel, good size. Crushing is easy work, and the bowl depth keeps all the substance in without any spills.

So, no, I really didn't need this, but I'm glad I got it.Solidly built(sturdy) and easy to useAt this price point, I wasn't really expecting much - I just liked that it had a plastic lid, and that it was made of metal, since the majority of the non-metal ones in the low price range all had reviews about the bowl breaking relatively easily.

Size was what I expected - I got a bit worried bc several reviews mentioned it was bigger than expected, but I didn't think it was- it's exactly how I imagined, a small but decent size, for personal use or cooking for 3-4 people. You can probably fit a cup of two of liquid in the bowl, to give you an idea of the scale. It's not super heavy but it has enough weight to it that it's not going to go flying if you use pressure when grinding.

It was surprisingly efficient for my needs: I needed to break down small, hard, pebbly pieces of rock and mineral, and occassionally plants, to pulverise into powder. I have tried several other methods and this was so much easier. Grinding works better than brute force or hammering the larger pieces, and I was able to pulverise it into a very fine powder relatively fast, just by grinding the pestle around.

There is a small rubber ring for gripping to the counter. I should mention that I have minimal use of my left arm, after a fractured humerus that healed improperly. This makes every day tasks very difficult, since I have no weight bearing ability. I was surprised how easily I was able to use this with just my right hand and arm, and how quickly it got the job done. You don't need a lot of strength to use this.

The lid was nice for keeping things from flying everywhere, but it does limit your range of motion when you start out, and blocks your view of the progress, so I took it off. Once most of the larger pieces of mineral and stone were powder, I popped the lid back on and resumed grinding it into a finer powder, and it was fine then.

The only complaint I have is that I lost a significant amount of the powder to the tip of the pestle, and the mortar bowl. At first I thought there was some kind of very fine, concentric/ribbed type surface to the bowl, but upon further inspection, there's not- it's very smooth. I tried to remove the powder stuck to the bowl and pestle with a small paint brush, then a new make-up brush for loose powder that I hadn't used yet, and finally my finger. None of them really did a good job of this.

Oddly, given the sticking, it cleans very easily - I wiped the inside out with a wet paper towel, which instantly removed the powder stuck to it, and then washed it like any other kitchen device. I'm not sure why the powder sticks and assume it just is a combination of the viscosity of the material you're grinding, and perhaps some kind of texture to the surface that is microscopic and not visible to the average eye.

Overall, a great little buy- perfect for seeds, nuts, plants, and even other hard, rocky substances you might need to break down, but on a small scale (I ended up with about 20 grams of pulverised powder, and there was still plenty of room - you could safely grind up to 40 grams of final powder output, without it being overfull. It's easier to do it in smaller increments though, so I would say 20 grams at a time, max, to make sure you get a good result (no chunky pieces) and so you don't have any spillage issues).

The only thing I *wouldn't* suggest it for is small pills or when you're using small amounts where every bit counts of whatever, bc you*will* lose some to the sides of the bowl and pestle. For example, it's a breeze to grind pills and medication, but if you're grinding a 20mg pill and you have to dose your pets with a very specific amount, it might not be the best solution, since your going to lose valuable medicine, and it's such a small amount that there's a cost loss factor. Perhaps a stiffer brush would remedy the issue; the brushes on my paint brush and new make up brush were not particularly firm. Otherwise, 5 stars. You can't beat the price, either.Very high quality item. Perfect for pill crushing. As my young grandson would say, “Heavy duty!”It's great for spices and crushing vitamins/pills. Good bowl size. It's sturdy and functional for most applications. ThanksBought this mortar & pestle to powder our elderly father's pills for mixing with applesauce twice daily. (He has trouble swallowing.) My wife also loves it for grinding herbs, and for measuring out 1-cup increments.

QUALITY/FUNCTIONALITY

We were surprised by the high quality and  excellent craftsmanship. The beautiful finish is satin smooth, no unsanitary grooves or milling marks. This mortar & pestle exudes class even as it grinds the toughest pills to fine dust in seconds, then easily washes clean.

This thing is SOLID. Feels like it's milled from a single block of fine steel. And HEAVY - like your average brick! Clearly built for a lifetime of use.

The thick rubber footing underneath prevents any slipping or sliding around. No more struggling to hold in place while pestling (grinding).

SIZE is just right. The mortar (bowl) looks like a short, heavy-duty, goblet, and holds exactly one cup (8 fluid ounces). The pestle (grinding stick) is an ideal length and girth for most hands, making it easy to use. Heavy enough to do the job quickly and well, but not enough to fatigue.

WHY STAINLESS STEEL?

Unlike the "cool" stone ones we've had, this SS mortar & pestle doesn't CHIP or BREAK when banged or knocked off the counter. It doesn't STAIN, and retains no faint ODOR from things previously ground. It leaves no RESIDUE to scrub from tiny crevasses and pores, so no GERMS get trapped in there to add unseen nasty to the next use.

It also looks great on the kitchen counter with our stainless steel appliances and accents.

Our highest recommendation. After having used two other stone mortar/Pestle sets, I was not happy with how the ends on the pestles would break down, leaving crushed stone mixed in my medicine. I no longer have to worry about that issue, with this stainless steel set.
As I said in the title, stainless steel is the way to go.The best! We had the glass type and was broken twice while crushing meds for one of our residents so we switched to this one and we can't be more pleased!I bought this back in 2016 and it still works great. I use it daily to crush spices and have had no issues. Easy to clean too.
reviews_1s:
I purchased this as a gift for my husband. By the time his birthday had rolled around, it was just passed the refund period. We discovered that there is a discoloration on the end of the pestle that is the same color as rust. (See pictures.) It has a slight texture, and we cannot get it off with cleaning. It just looks dirty. It's very frustrating since I was quite excited to give this gift and it was a disappointment.

We attempted to contact the manufacturer by visiting their website and filling out the form provided in order to send our contact information and our concern to them. The form never worked, and we tried several different internet browsers and several different days spanning a week period. (Just in case it was a server issue.) We even registered our email with them and never received a confirmation email. I feel like we tried everything we could.

I like the actual product and would be happy to provide a higher rating if I had a clean, unblemished version. I just can't find anyone to contact!This is scam product, not stainless steel. Broke while using it, it is not stainless steel and is filled with cement. Please don’t buy this product . It’s a SCAM.This doesn't work as well as I thought. Because it's so smooth inside, it's hard to grind spices up. It just moved around. I think the rough material in other mortar gives it friction and would do a better job. Plus having mortars with flat, wide interiors that are 3-4 inches in diameter allow spices to spread out and makes it more efficient in grinding it into smaller pieces. This does not do either one.This might be coated in stainless steel, but its made out of something completely different. The bottom isn't even sealed, just has a crappy sticker that isn't even stuck on all the way so you can see the weird cement inside. Don't buy this!!Can’t grind big portions. Can only grind single piece of garlic or green chili and not more than that at the same time. Not worth itI would have given this zero stars if possible. I'm shocked it's received so many good reviews. I tried to mash up garlic, chillis, and ginger and it was nearly impossible. Because it's stainless steel all of the ingredients were sliding all over the place, even out of the bowl! I got so frustrated I just dumped out the ingredients and chopped them up with a knife. After doing some research I learned that a good mortar and pestle should be rough on the inside so it traps the ingredients. What a total waste.I bought this based on the ratings and reviews. This product was smaller than I had imagined. It also had a metallic film in the mortar with a smal chip in it. The pestle base is not wide enough to crush anything bigger than a tic tac. The mortar surface is too smooth and I can see it would be difficult to crush like garlic or ginger or even pills without it shooting out of the mortar. I was not happy with the purchase at all I returned the same day I received it.I'm not sure how this is so well rated. I've used this once, and the sides are scratched. I used it to mix micas and a mineral foundation base, and the sides scratched from using it as intended. Every ingredient is face safe, and a small particle size, so nothing sharp or oddly shaped. I'm not happy, but with its low price, I guess what you pay for.Very poor craftmanship. The inside had a sharp, round dent where medicine could get caught up in. The box was flimsy, and it is made in China. This is NOT a good quality morter. I read most of the reviews, and they were positive despite the fact that the manufacturing of this product is done in China. So, I gave it a try...and now I'm sending it back. It has a dent! Where is quality control ???!!!I have tried to use it many times now but found it not good for Indian cooking. Can't be used to make fine pastes or crush dry ingredients well, either things splatter or stick to the sides, and the pestle oftentimes slips to the bottom, and you end up being frustrated.
